cd "/lustre/fast/fast/sgoel/forecasting/current_sim/final_runs_v37/claude_code_aljazeeraQ12026v37_glm52_r00/26-06-14-19-17-03/agents/minimalHarness_glm-52[1m]_001/workspace"
ls *.json 2>/dev/null; echo "---state---"; cat state.json 2>/dev/null | head -30
echo "---predictions dir latest---"; ls predictions/ 2>/dev/null | tail -5

cd "/lustre/fast/fast/sgoel/forecasting/current_sim/final_runs_v37/claude_code_aljazeeraQ12026v37_glm52_r00/26-06-14-19-17-03/agents/minimalHarness_glm-52[1m]_001/workspace"
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
python3 << 'EOF'
import csv
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].strip() in ('False','')]
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].strip() in ('True','true','1',) or (r['ground_truth'].strip() and r['ground_truth'].strip()!='')]
# careful: is_resolved col
print("Total:",len(rows),"is_resolved True:",sum(1 for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].strip()=='True'))
pred=sum(1 for r in rows if r['my_prediction'].strip())
print("With my_prediction:",pred)
# soonest resolving active
from collections import Counter
# find questions resolving Jan 9-15
print("\n=== Active questions resolving Jan 9-15 ===")
for r in sorted(active,key=lambda x:x['resolution_date']):
    if r['resolution_date']<='2026-01-15':
        print(f"[{r['resolution_date']}] {r['qid']}: {r['title'][:75]}")
EOF

blocks.text: I'm at 2026-01-09 with 31 resolved, 327 active forecasts. Last batch taught me candidate SETS must include non-obvious answers (Oresar = Lviv, Morocco SF = Nigeria, trade = Taiwan). Two questions resolve tomorrow (Jan 10). Given the People's Party won the Thai party-list poll, let me shift the PM-survey forecast toward Pita, and broaden the Iran-province one. Let me also check the AFCON QF/SF bracket for the final-related questions.

input.command:
cd "/lustre/fast/fast/sgoel/forecasting/current_sim/final_runs_v37/claude_code_aljazeeraQ12026v37_glm52_r00/26-06-14-19-17-03/agents/minimalHarness_glm-52[1m]_001/workspace"
echo "=== AFCON QF (Jan 9-10) + SF bracket ==="
for d in 09 10; do
  f="articles/2026/01/$d/articles.jsonl"
  [ -f "$f" ] && echo "--Jan $d--" && grep -ohiE "[A-Za-z ]+ [0-9]+-[0-9]+ [A-Za-z ]+" "$f" 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "Morocco|Cameroon|Nigeria|Egypt|Algeria|Ivory|Senegal|Mali" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
done
